text:
+ chronic leading to poor use of iron by the body
Defect in the absorption of iron or certain vitamins
(vitamin B12, vitamin B9) in diseases of the
small intestine (severe and/or extensive and/or operated)
or in case of associated celiac disease
= Lack of iron or vitamin B9 (or folic acid) intake when diet is insufficient
æ Consumption of vitamin B12 by an excess of
bacteria in the small intestine in cases of strictures
or small intestine surgery
+ Excess consumption of folic acid during
certain treatments such as Methotrexate
Toxicity of certain drugs (Azathioprine,
Purinethol, Methotrexate) at the level of the bone
marrow (which makes blood cells).

Iron deficiency is defined almost exclusively
by the serum ferritin level, which varies according to
general inflammation. It is accepted that levels should be
maintained > 30 µg/L in the absence of serum inflammation and >
100 µg/L in case of inflammation. It is estimated that 45% of
IBD patients suffer from iron deficiency (notably
during the first year after diagnosis).
